name: File System Journaling
description: Journaling keeps a file system consistent across crashes: before changing the file system, the intended updates are written to a journal and committed durably, after which the changes are checkpointed to their final locations; if a crash occurs before checkpointing, recovery replays the committed journal entries.

graph TD
    N1["File operation request"]
    N2["Begin transaction"]
    N3["Write intent to journal"]
    N4["Commit to journal"]
    N5{"Journal durable?"}
    N6["Retry / abort"]
    N7["Checkpoint to FS"]
    N8["Update metadata+data"]
    N9["Mark entry done"]
    N10{"Crash before checkpoint?"}
    N11["Replay on recovery"]
    N12["Consistent file system"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|no| N6
    N6 -->|retry| N3
    N5 -->|yes| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 -->|yes| N11
    N11 --> N12
    N10 -->|no| N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
